verses.text: Israel mourns that a tribe is nearly cut off. Mourning. The cost of war is felt.
Israel mourns that a tribe is nearly cut off. Mourning. The cost of war is felt.
Israel mourns that a tribe is nearly cut off. Mourning. The cost of war is felt.
Israel mourns that a tribe is nearly cut off. Mourning. The cost of war is felt.
They had sworn not to give their daughters to Benjamin. The oath. A rash vow binds them.
They had sworn not to give their daughters to Benjamin. The oath. A rash vow binds them.
They had sworn not to give their daughters to Benjamin. The oath. A rash vow binds them.
They find Jabesh-gilead came not, and give its virgins. Jabesh. Wives are sought.
They find Jabesh-gilead came not, and give its virgins. Jabesh. Wives are sought.
They find Jabesh-gilead came not, and give its virgins. Jabesh. Wives are sought.
They find Jabesh-gilead came not, and give its virgins. Jabesh. Wives are sought.
They find Jabesh-gilead came not, and give its virgins. Jabesh. Wives are sought.
Peace is offered to Benjamin's remnant. Peace. They seek to restore the tribe.
Peace is offered to Benjamin's remnant. Peace. They seek to restore the tribe.
Peace is offered to Benjamin's remnant. Peace. They seek to restore the tribe.
But the wives are still too few. Too few. The oath leaves a gap.
But the wives are still too few. Too few. The oath leaves a gap.
But the wives are still too few. Too few. The oath leaves a gap.
They allow them to catch the daughters of Shiloh. Shiloh. A way is found.
They allow them to catch the daughters of Shiloh. Shiloh. A way is found.
They allow them to catch the daughters of Shiloh. Shiloh. A way is found.
The Benjamites take wives and rebuild their cities. Restored. The tribe survives.
The Benjamites take wives and rebuild their cities. Restored. The tribe survives.
All return home; 'every man did that which was right in his own eyes.' No king. The book ends in disorder, longing for a king.
